변경 기록

날짜 변경
2026-06-27 WP52: ≥12 KB expansion — RepoMap pipeline, format_chat_chunks, .aider.conf.yml
2026-06-27 WP42: source citations + parity blocks
2026-06-27 WP27: initial ai axis doc with source citations

snapshot_to_current 델타

Area Snapshot Current
Context injection RepoMap + convention files via read: Prompt class count and chunk assembly evolve per release
Skill analogue Documented as absent Third-party SKILL.md packs remain unsupported natively
Tag cache .aider.tags.cache.v3/v4 CACHE_VERSION bumps on tree-sitter pack changes
map_mul_no_files Default 8 in RepoMap, CLI multiplier default 2 Verify args.map_multiplier_no_files wiring on upgrade
22

축 05: 서브에이전트

AXIS

005_subagents-collab/05_ai_subagent.md

기초 설명

Aider's closest collaboration primitive is architect mode: one model (architect) drafts an implementation plan in natural language; after optional human approval, a nested Coder.create() session spins up a second editor model that applies concrete edits. AskCoder provides a read-only analysis mode without spawning a second agent. All handoffs are sequential, in-process, sharing the same io REPL — not multi-agent orchestration.

이 문서에서 다루는 것

  • Architecture
    1. Coder class registry (coders/__init__.py)
    1. Coder.create() — nested session factory (base_coder.py:125-201)
  • 2.1 Resolution order
  • 2.2 Context carry-over from from_coder (:153-187)
  • 2.3 Unknown format
    1. AskCoder — read-only sub-mode (ask_coder.py, ask_prompts.py)
  • 3.1 Class definition
  • 3.2 Entry points
  • 3.3 _generic_chat_command flow (commands.py:1206-1230)
    1. ArchitectCoder — full dual-model flow (architect_coder.py)
  • 4.1 Class hierarchy

소스 코드 스니펫

README / docs

flowchart TD
  User["User /architect or --architect"] --> AC["ArchitectCoder<br/>edit_format=architect"]
  AC --> LLM1["main_model (architect)<br/>ArchitectPrompts"]
  LLM1 --> Plan["partial_response_content<br/>change plan text"]
  Plan --> Gate{"auto_accept_architect<br/>or confirm_ask?"}
  Gate -->|declined| Stop["return — no edits"]
  Gate -->|approved| Create["Coder.create(from_coder=self)<br/>main_model=editor_model"]
  Create --> EC["Editor coder<br/>editor-diff / etc."]
  EC --> Run["editor_coder.run(with_message=plan)"]
  Run --> Merge["move_back_cur_messages()<br/>sync cost + commit hashes"]
  Ask["/ask → AskCoder"] --> LLM2["main_model<br/>AskPrompts — no edits"]

README / docs

# 110_aider/aider/coders/__init__.py:18-34
__all__ = [
    HelpCoder, AskCoder, Coder, EditBlockCoder, ...
    ArchitectCoder,
    EditorEditBlockCoder, EditorWholeFileCoder, EditorDiffFencedCoder,
    ContextCoder,
]

README / docs

# 110_aider/aider/coders/base_coder.py:125-148
@classmethod
def create(self, main_model=None, edit_format=None, io=None, from_coder=None, ...):
    if not main_model:
        main_model = from_coder.main_model if from_coder else models.Model(...)
    if edit_format is None:
        edit_format = from_coder.edit_format if from_coder else main_model.edit_format
    if not io and from_coder:
        io = from_coder.io

README / docs

# 110_aider/aider/coders/ask_coder.py:5-9
class AskCoder(Coder):
    """Ask questions about code without making any changes."""
    edit_format = "ask"
    gpt_prompts = AskPrompts()

README / docs

# 110_aider/aider/coders/ask_prompts.py:7-18
main_system = """Act as an expert code analyst.
Answer questions about the supplied code.
...
"""
overeager_prompt = """Do not return fully detailed code or full diffs.
Describe the needed changes or give a plan.
"""

README / docs

coder = Coder.create(
    io=self.io,
    from_coder=self.coder,
    edit_format=edit_format,
    summarize_from_coder=False,
)
coder.run(user_msg)
raise SwitchCoder(from_coder=coder, ...)  # restore prior mode after one-shot

README / docs

# 110_aider/aider/coders/architect_coder.py:6-9
class ArchitectCoder(AskCoder):
    edit_format = "architect"
    gpt_prompts = ArchitectPrompts()
    auto_accept_architect = False  # class default; overridden by __init__ kwarg

README / docs

main_system = """Act as an expert architect engineer and provide direction to your editor engineer.
Study the change request and the current code.
Describe how to modify the code to complete the request.
The editor engineer will rely solely on your instructions, so make them unambiguous and complete.
...
DO NOT show the entire updated function/file/etc!
"""

README / docs

def reply_completed(self):
    content = self.partial_response_content
    if not content or not content.strip():
        return

    if not self.auto_accept_architect and not self.io.confirm_ask("Edit the files?"):
        return

    editor_model = self.main_model.editor_model or self.main_model

    kwargs["main_model"] = editor_model
    kwargs["edit_format"] = self.main_model.editor_edit_format
    kwargs["suggest_shell_commands"] = False
    kwargs["map_tokens"] = 0
    kwargs["total_cost"] = self.total_cost
    kwargs["cache_prompts"] = False
    kwargs["num_cache_warming_pings"] = 0
    kwargs["summarize_from_coder"] = False

    new_kwargs = dict(io=self.io, from_coder=self)
    new_kwargs.update(kwargs)

    editor_coder = Coder.create(**new_kwargs)
    editor_coder.cur_messages = []
    editor_coder.done_messages = []

    editor_coder.run(with_message=content, preproc=False)

    self.move_back_cur_messages("I made those changes to the files.")
    self.total_cost = editor_coder.total_cost
    self.aider_commit_hashes = editor_coder.aider_commit_hashes

README / docs

def move_back_cur_messages(self, message):
    self.done_messages += self.cur_messages
    self.summarize_start()
    if message:
        self.done_messages += [
            dict(role="user", content=message),
            dict(role="assistant", content="Ok."),
        ]
    self.cur_messages = []

README / docs

output = (
    f"Editor model: {main_model.editor_model.name} with"
    f" {main_model.editor_edit_format} edit format"
)

README / docs

# Pattern seen throughout model-settings.yml
edit_format: architect
editor_model_name: gpt-4o
editor_edit_format: editor-diff

README / docs

def cmd_ask(self, args):
    return self._generic_chat_command(args, "ask")

def cmd_architect(self, args):
    return self._generic_chat_command(args, "architect")

코퍼스 — 005_subagents-collab/05_ai_subagent.md (심화)

Aider — Subagents / Collaboration

분석 스냅샷: 110_aider/ — dual-model architect → editor pipeline in architect_coder.py; AskCoder for read-only Q&A; no parallel subagent scheduler, no worker pool, no cross-session agent IDs.

Aider's closest collaboration primitive is architect mode: one model (architect) drafts an implementation plan in natural language; after optional human approval, a nested Coder.create() session spins up a second editor model that applies concrete edits. AskCoder provides a read-only analysis mode without spawning a second agent. All handoffs are sequential, in-process, sharing the same io REPL — not multi-agent orchestration.

Architecture

flowchart TD
  User["User /architect or --architect"] --> AC["ArchitectCoder<br/>edit_format=architect"]
  AC --> LLM1["main_model (architect)<br/>ArchitectPrompts"]
  LLM1 --> Plan["partial_response_content<br/>change plan text"]
  Plan --> Gate{"auto_accept_architect<br/>or confirm_ask?"}
  Gate -->|declined| Stop["return — no edits"]
  Gate -->|approved| Create["Coder.create(from_coder=self)<br/>main_model=editor_model"]
  Create --> EC["Editor coder<br/>editor-diff / etc."]
  EC --> Run["editor_coder.run(with_message=plan)"]
  Run --> Merge["move_back_cur_messages()<br/>sync cost + commit hashes"]
  Ask["/ask → AskCoder"] --> LLM2["main_model<br/>AskPrompts — no edits"]

1. Coder class registry (coders/__init__.py)

All edit formats are plain Python subclasses registered by edit_format string:

# 110_aider/aider/coders/__init__.py:18-34
__all__ = [
    HelpCoder, AskCoder, Coder, EditBlockCoder, ...
    ArchitectCoder,
    EditorEditBlockCoder, EditorWholeFileCoder, EditorDiffFencedCoder,
    ContextCoder,
]

Coder.create() (base_coder.py:190-194) iterates __all__, matches coder.edit_format == edit_format, instantiates. No plugin loader, no TOML agent definitions — format string IS the role selector.

Class edit_format Role
AskCoder ask Read-only Q&A
ArchitectCoder architect Plan-only; spawns editor
EditBlockCoder diff Standard edits
EditorEditBlockCoder editor-diff Editor pass for architect
ContextCoder context Surrounding code context view

2. Coder.create() — nested session factory (base_coder.py:125-201)

2.1 Resolution order

# 110_aider/aider/coders/base_coder.py:125-148
@classmethod
def create(self, main_model=None, edit_format=None, io=None, from_coder=None, ...):
    if not main_model:
        main_model = from_coder.main_model if from_coder else models.Model(...)
    if edit_format is None:
        edit_format = from_coder.edit_format if from_coder else main_model.edit_format
    if not io and from_coder:
        io = from_coder.io

2.2 Context carry-over from from_coder (:153-187)

When switching or nesting coders:

Copied field Purpose
fnames, read_only_fnames Same file context
done_messages, cur_messages Chat continuity
aider_commit_hashes Undo scope
commands (cloned) Slash commands
total_cost, token counters Billing aggregate
file_watcher File change detection

If edit_format changes and history exists, summarize_all() may compress done_messages to avoid confusing the new LLM with old edit-format examples (:161-168).

Architect editor pass sets summarize_from_coder=False explicitly (architect_coder.py:32) so architect plan stays in cur_messages for the editor's with_message injection.

2.3 Unknown format

Raises UnknownEditFormat with list of valid formats (:196-201).

3. AskCoder — read-only sub-mode (ask_coder.py, ask_prompts.py)

3.1 Class definition

# 110_aider/aider/coders/ask_coder.py:5-9
class AskCoder(Coder):
    """Ask questions about code without making any changes."""
    edit_format = "ask"
    gpt_prompts = AskPrompts()

Inherits full Coder loop but prompts forbid substantive edits:

# 110_aider/aider/coders/ask_prompts.py:7-18
main_system = """Act as an expert code analyst.
Answer questions about the supplied code.
...
"""
overeager_prompt = """Do not return fully detailed code or full diffs.
Describe the needed changes or give a plan.
"""

No reply_completed override — AskCoder never spawns a nested editor. Single-model, single-pass.

3.2 Entry points

Entry Path Behavior
/ask [prompt] commands.py:1182-1184 _generic_chat_command(args, "ask")
/ask alone commands.py:1207-1209 cmd_chat_mode("ask") — persistent mode switch
--chat-mode ask args.py:165-169 Startup format

3.3 _generic_chat_command flow (commands.py:1206-1230)

coder = Coder.create(
    io=self.io,
    from_coder=self.coder,
    edit_format=edit_format,
    summarize_from_coder=False,
)
coder.run(user_msg)
raise SwitchCoder(from_coder=coder, ...)  # restore prior mode after one-shot

One-shot /ask question runs AskCoder for a single turn, then raises SwitchCoder to revert to previous edit format without summarizing — keeping architect/code mode context intact.

4. ArchitectCoder — full dual-model flow (architect_coder.py)

4.1 Class hierarchy

# 110_aider/aider/coders/architect_coder.py:6-9
class ArchitectCoder(AskCoder):
    edit_format = "architect"
    gpt_prompts = ArchitectPrompts()
    auto_accept_architect = False  # class default; overridden by __init__ kwarg

Extends AskCoder (read-only planning DNA) but overrides reply_completed to trigger editor pass.

4.2 Architect system prompt (architect_prompts.py:7-17)

main_system = """Act as an expert architect engineer and provide direction to your editor engineer.
Study the change request and the current code.
Describe how to modify the code to complete the request.
The editor engineer will rely solely on your instructions, so make them unambiguous and complete.
...
DO NOT show the entire updated function/file/etc!
"""

Architect output is instructions, not fenced edit blocks — editor model interprets plan text.

4.3 reply_completed() — complete handoff (architect_coder.py:11-48)

def reply_completed(self):
    content = self.partial_response_content
    if not content or not content.strip():
        return

    if not self.auto_accept_architect and not self.io.confirm_ask("Edit the files?"):
        return

    editor_model = self.main_model.editor_model or self.main_model

    kwargs["main_model"] = editor_model
    kwargs["edit_format"] = self.main_model.editor_edit_format
    kwargs["suggest_shell_commands"] = False
    kwargs["map_tokens"] = 0
    kwargs["total_cost"] = self.total_cost
    kwargs["cache_prompts"] = False
    kwargs["num_cache_warming_pings"] = 0
    kwargs["summarize_from_coder"] = False

    new_kwargs = dict(io=self.io, from_coder=self)
    new_kwargs.update(kwargs)

    editor_coder = Coder.create(**new_kwargs)
    editor_coder.cur_messages = []
    editor_coder.done_messages = []

    editor_coder.run(with_message=content, preproc=False)

    self.move_back_cur_messages("I made those changes to the files.")
    self.total_cost = editor_coder.total_cost
    self.aider_commit_hashes = editor_coder.aider_commit_hashes

Step-by-step semantics:

Step Lines Meaning
Empty plan guard :14-15 No editor spawn if architect silent
Human gate :17-18 confirm_ask("Edit the files?") unless auto-accept
Editor model pick :22-23 main_model.editor_model or fallback to architect model
Editor format :26 From main_model.editor_edit_format (e.g. editor-diff)
Disable shell :27 Editor should not suggest shell during apply
Disable repo map :28 map_tokens=0 — editor has full file bodies in chat
Disable cache warming :30-31 Nested pass skips prompt cache pings
Fresh message lists :38-39 Editor starts clean cur/done; plan via with_message
preproc=False :44 Skip re-processing plan as user slash commands
Merge back :46-48 Append synthetic user/assistant pair; sync cost/commits

4.4 move_back_cur_messages() (base_coder.py:1036-1046)

def move_back_cur_messages(self, message):
    self.done_messages += self.cur_messages
    self.summarize_start()
    if message:
        self.done_messages += [
            dict(role="user", content=message),
            dict(role="assistant", content="Ok."),
        ]
    self.cur_messages = []

Architect session folds editor turn into durable history with acknowledgment stub — same pattern as repo map injection.

4.5 Announcements (base_coder.py:239-244)

When edit_format == "architect", startup banner shows both models:

output = (
    f"Editor model: {main_model.editor_model.name} with"
    f" {main_model.editor_edit_format} edit format"
)

5. Editor model configuration (models.py)

5.1 Model metadata fields

  • editor_model_name, editor_edit_format on ModelInfo (models.py:145-146).
  • get_editor_model() (:625-645): resolves separate Model instance; prefixes editor- onto diff/whole formats when unset.

5.2 Bundled defaults (resources/model-settings.yml)

Many models ship with architect pairing, e.g.:

# Pattern seen throughout model-settings.yml
edit_format: architect
editor_model_name: gpt-4o
editor_edit_format: editor-diff

CLI overrides: --editor-model, --editor-edit-format (args.py:194+).

5.3 Sanity check (models.py:1155-1159)

On startup, if editor_model differs from main and weak models, validates editor model connectivity.

6. CLI and slash-command entry

6.1 Flags (args.py:171-183)

Flag Effect
--architect Sets edit_format=architect (store_const)
--auto-accept-architect BooleanOptionalAction, default True at argparse level
--editor-model Override editor LLM
--chat-mode Includes architect, ask in choices

Note: ArchitectCoder.auto_accept_architect = False at class level, but base_coder.__init__ receives auto_accept_architect=args.auto_accept_architect from main (main.py:1005) — instance attribute wins. Effective default for CLI sessions is auto-accept True unless user passes --no-auto-accept-architect.

6.2 Slash commands (commands.py)

def cmd_ask(self, args):
    return self._generic_chat_command(args, "ask")

def cmd_architect(self, args):
    return self._generic_chat_command(args, "architect")

Persistent mode: /architect with no args → cmd_chat_mode("architect").

Related: /code returns to main edit format (:1186-1188); /ok aliases approval message to code mode (:1198-1204).

7. What Aider does NOT implement

Feature Codex / multi-agent harnesses Aider
Parallel subagents multi_agent_v2, fan-out Not implemented
Task tool / spawn task tool + thread manager Single nested Coder.create()
Agent IDs / mailboxes Cross-session messaging Shared io, one REPL
Worker pool Background employees None
Subagent specialization TOML agents, roles edit_format class only
Autonomous handoff Model decides to spawn Architect always triggers editor after gate (if plan non-empty)

External orchestration (jaw employees, custom scripts) required for true multi-agent parallelism.

8. Comparison: Ask vs Architect vs Code

Mode Class Edits files? Second model? Human gate
ask AskCoder No No N/A
architect ArchitectCoder Via editor Yes confirm_ask or auto-accept
diff / etc. EditBlockCoder… Yes No Normal edit flow

Architect inherits AskCoder's planning-oriented prompts but adds reply_completed editor pipeline. Editor coders (Editor*Coder) are never user-selected directly — only via architect handoff or --chat-mode for debugging.

9. ContextCoder — related single-agent mode

ContextCoder (context_coder.py:17-18) forces repo_map.refresh = "always" and multiplies map tokens — a context-expansion mode, not a subagent. Documented here because /context sits beside /ask and /architect in the mode-switch family (commands.py:1194-1196).

10. Parity matrix vs Codex (co)

Concern Codex (co) Aider (ai)
Subagent spawn task tool + thread manager Nested single Coder.create() only
Parallelism multi_agent_v2 config Not implemented
Roles TOML agents / spawn metadata architect → editor model swap
Read-only agent Subagent with readonly flag AskCoder (edit_format=ask)
Approval gate Policy / guardian io.confirm_ask("Edit the files?")
Cost accounting Per-thread Merged into parent total_cost
Session isolation Separate threads Cleared cur_messages on editor; shared io

11. Operational workflow (user perspective)

Architect session

  1. User starts aider --architect or /architect refactor the auth module
  2. Banner shows architect model + editor model (get_announcements)
  3. Architect model responds with plan text (no auto-applied edits)
  4. Unless --no-auto-accept-architect, user sees Edit the files? [Y/n]
  5. On approval, editor model runs with plan as with_message; diffs applied
  6. History records "I made those changes to the files." / "Ok."
  7. User continues in architect mode for next request

Ask session

  1. /ask how does RepoMap rank files?
  2. AskCoder answers without edit blocks or editor spawn
  3. SwitchCoder restores prior mode if one-shot

12. Token and context implications

  • Editor pass sets map_tokens=0 — relies on files already in chat, not repo map (architect_coder.py:28).
  • Editor cur_messages and done_messages cleared — plan arrives only via run(with_message=content); prior architect turn messages moved to parent after merge.
  • summarize_from_coder=False on both architect spawn and slash one-shots — avoids losing plan detail to summarizer.

13. Evidence quality bar

  • Full architect_coder.reply_completed path with line numbers
  • Coder.create factory and from_coder carry-over
  • AskCoder read-only boundary documented
  • Negative claim: no parallel agents
  • CLI/slash entry points with args defaults
  • Parity table vs Codex subagents

14. Source citation index

Path Lines Role

110_aider/aider/coders/ask_coder.py 5-9 AskCoder class
110_aider/aider/coders/ask_prompts.py 6-39 Ask system prompts
110_aider/aider/coders/architect_prompts.py 6-38 Architect system prompts
110_aider/aider/coders/base_coder.py 125-201, 239-244, 1036-1046 Coder.create, announcements, move_back_cur_messages
110_aider/aider/coders/__init__.py 18-34 Coder registry
110_aider/aider/coders/context_coder.py 17-18 Context mode map refresh
110_aider/aider/commands.py 1182-1230 /ask, /architect, _generic_chat_command
110_aider/aider/args.py 165-183, 194+ --architect, --auto-accept-architect, --editor-model
110_aider/aider/main.py 973-1006 Coder.create startup kwargs

Related corpus docs

  • Codex subagents: 005_subagents-collab/05_co_subagent.md
  • Superpowers (methodology-only): 005_subagents-collab/05_sp_subagent.md
  • Skill/context (RepoMap, not agents): 004_skill-system/04_ai_skill.md
  • Guardian / confirm: 012_guardian-approval/12_ai_guardian.md
  • Overview: 000_overview/00_ai_overview.md

변경 기록

날짜 변경
2026-06-27 WP52: ≥12 KB expansion — architect flow, Coder.create, AskCoder, parity
2026-06-27 WP42: source citations + parity blocks
2026-06-27 WP27: initial ai axis doc

snapshot_to_current 델타

Area Snapshot Current
Architect/editor Dual-model handoff in v1 corpus Editor model selection flags may expand per release
Parallelism Absent by design External orchestration required for fan-out
auto_accept_architect argparse default True vs class False Confirm effective default on upgrade
Editor formats editor-diff, editor-whole, etc. New editor-* coders may appear in __all__
23

축 06: 앱·서버 표면

AXIS

006_app-server-client-surfaces/06_ai_surface.md

기초 설명

Aider는 앱서버가 없는 순수 CLI 도구다. Codex의 app-server + protocol + client 삼각형과 근본적으로 다르며, Claude Code처럼 단일 프로세스 아키텍처이되, 외형은 더 단순하다. 장기 실행 서버, wire protocol, SDK, IDE 확장이 모두 존재하지 않는다. 유일한 서비스 표면은 대화형 CLI이고, 선택적으로 Streamlit 기반 웹 GUI를 올릴 수 있다.

이 문서에서 다루는 것

    1. 진입점과 부트스트랩
  • 1.1 main.py — 부트스트랩 체인
  • 1.2 Coder 클래스 — 핵심 실행 단위
    1. 클라이언트 표면
  • 2.1 대화형 CLI (기본 모드)
  • 2.2 Streamlit GUI (--gui)
  • 2.3 파이프 모드 (비대화형)
  • 2.4 File Watcher 모드
  • 2.5 ClipboardWatcher
    1. LLM 통합 (LiteLLM)
  • 3.1 다중 제공사 지원
  • 3.2 편집 포맷 전략

소스 코드 스니펫

README / docs

graph TD
    subgraph Process["단일 Python 프로세스"]
        Main["main.py\n(args 파서 + 부트스트랩)"]
        Coder["Coder 인스턴스\n(base_coder.py)"]
        Commands["Commands\n(/ 명령어)"]
        IO["InputOutput\n(prompt_toolkit)"]
        LiteLLM["LiteLLM\n(다중 제공사)"]
        Git["GitRepo\n(gitpython)"]
    end

    subgraph Surfaces["클라이언트 표면"]
        CLI["대화형 CLI\n(prompt_toolkit)"]
        GUI["Streamlit GUI\n(gui.py, 선택적)"]
        Pipe["파이프 모드\n(--yes-always)"]
        Watch["File Watcher\n(--watch-files)"]
    end

    Main --> Coder
    Coder --> Commands
    Coder --> IO
    Coder --> LiteLLM
    Coder --> Git
    CLI --> Main
    GUI --> Main
    Pipe --> Main
    Watch --> Main

README / docs

1. Git 저장소 감지 (get_git_root)
2. 설정 파일 탐색 (.aider.conf.yml)
3. ArgumentParser 생성 (get_parser)
4. .env 로드 (load_dotenv_files)
5. 인수 검증 및 환경 변수 설정
6. Analytics 초기화
7. GUI 모드 분기 (--gui)
8. Git 설정 (setup_git)
9. 모델 레지스트리 로딩 (register_models, register_litellm_models)
10. 모델 선택 (select_default_model)
11. Coder 인스턴스 생성
12. 대화 루프 시작

README / docs

classDiagram
    class Coder {
        +run(user_message)
        +run_one(user_message)
        +send_message(messages)
        +apply_edits(edits)
        +get_context_from_history()
        +get_repo_map()
    }
    class EditBlockCoder
    class WholeCoder
    class UnifiedDiffCoder
    class ArchitectCoder

    Coder <|-- EditBlockCoder
    Coder <|-- WholeCoder
    Coder <|-- UnifiedDiffCoder
    Coder <|-- ArchitectCoder

README / docs

# 110_aider/io.py — InputOutput 핵심
class InputOutput:
    def __init__(self, pretty, yes_always, ...):
        self.pretty = pretty
        self.yes_always = yes_always

    def get_input(self, ...):
        # prompt_toolkit 기반 사용자 입력
        # 자동완성, 히스토리, 편집 모드 지원

    def confirm_ask(self, question, ...):
        # 사용자 확인 (yes_always이면 자동 승인)

    def tool_output(self, msg, ...):
        # 컬러 코드 적용 출력

    def tool_error(self, msg, ...):
        # 에러 출력 (빨간색)

README / docs

launch_gui():
  1. write_streamlit_credentials() — 이메일 프롬프트 비활성화
  2. streamlit.web.cli.main(["run", gui.__file__, ...])
  3. 브라우저 자동 열기

README / docs

echo "fix the bug in main.py" | aider --yes-always --no-git

README / docs

# 110_aider/models.py — 모델 레지스트리
class ModelSettings:
    # 모델별 설정: edit_format, use_repo_map, send_undo_reply, ...

# 모델 선택 우선순위:
#   1. --model CLI 인수
#   2. AIDER_MODEL 환경변수
#   3. 사용 가능한 API 키에서 추론
#   4. OpenRouter OAuth 제안

README / docs

자동 커밋 흐름:
  1. LLM이 편집 응답 반환
  2. Coder가 편집 적용
  3. 변경된 파일 자동 스테이징
  4. aider 포맷 커밋 메시지 생성
  5. 자동 커밋 (--auto-commits 기본 활성)

README / docs

CLI/GUI --model / AIDER_MODEL
    → models.Model (name, edit_format, repo_map flags)
    → models.py:litellm.completion(**kwargs)
    → LiteLLM router (OPENAI_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENROUTER_API_KEY, …)
    → upstream HTTP

README / docs

# gui.py:77-84
io = CaptureIO(pretty=False, yes=True, dry_run=coder.io.dry_run, ...)
coder.commands.io = io  # commands 경로만 교체; coder.io는 유지

코퍼스 — 006_app-server-client-surfaces/06_ai_surface.md (심화)

Aider 앱·서버·클라이언트 서피스 분석

분석 스냅샷: 110_aider/ — app/server/client surfaces.

⚠️ 오픈소스 분석: 이 문서는 110_aider/ 저장소의 main.py, coders/base_coder.py, commands.py, io.py, gui.py 소스 코드에 기반한다.

Aider는 앱서버가 없는 순수 CLI 도구다. Codex의 app-server + protocol + client 삼각형과 근본적으로 다르며, Claude Code처럼 단일 프로세스 아키텍처이되, 외형은 더 단순하다. 장기 실행 서버, wire protocol, SDK, IDE 확장이 모두 존재하지 않는다. 유일한 서비스 표면은 대화형 CLI이고, 선택적으로 Streamlit 기반 웹 GUI를 올릴 수 있다.

graph TD
    subgraph Process["단일 Python 프로세스"]
        Main["main.py\n(args 파서 + 부트스트랩)"]
        Coder["Coder 인스턴스\n(base_coder.py)"]
        Commands["Commands\n(/ 명령어)"]
        IO["InputOutput\n(prompt_toolkit)"]
        LiteLLM["LiteLLM\n(다중 제공사)"]
        Git["GitRepo\n(gitpython)"]
    end

    subgraph Surfaces["클라이언트 표면"]
        CLI["대화형 CLI\n(prompt_toolkit)"]
        GUI["Streamlit GUI\n(gui.py, 선택적)"]
        Pipe["파이프 모드\n(--yes-always)"]
        Watch["File Watcher\n(--watch-files)"]
    end

    Main --> Coder
    Coder --> Commands
    Coder --> IO
    Coder --> LiteLLM
    Coder --> Git
    CLI --> Main
    GUI --> Main
    Pipe --> Main
    Watch --> Main

1. 진입점과 부트스트랩

1.1 main.py — 부트스트랩 체인

main() 함수가 전체 부트스트랩을 관리한다. 1,275줄의 단일 함수로, 다음 순서를 따른다 (신뢰도 🟢 높음):

1. Git 저장소 감지 (get_git_root)
2. 설정 파일 탐색 (.aider.conf.yml)
3. ArgumentParser 생성 (get_parser)
4. .env 로드 (load_dotenv_files)
5. 인수 검증 및 환경 변수 설정
6. Analytics 초기화
7. GUI 모드 분기 (--gui)
8. Git 설정 (setup_git)
9. 모델 레지스트리 로딩 (register_models, register_litellm_models)
10. 모델 선택 (select_default_model)
11. Coder 인스턴스 생성
12. 대화 루프 시작

1.2 Coder 클래스 — 핵심 실행 단위

Coder 클래스(coders/base_coder.py)가 Aider의 핵심 실행 단위다. 편집 포맷(diff, whole, udiff, architect)에 따라 서브클래스로 분기한다.

classDiagram
    class Coder {
        +run(user_message)
        +run_one(user_message)
        +send_message(messages)
        +apply_edits(edits)
        +get_context_from_history()
        +get_repo_map()
    }
    class EditBlockCoder
    class WholeCoder
    class UnifiedDiffCoder
    class ArchitectCoder

    Coder <|-- EditBlockCoder
    Coder <|-- WholeCoder
    Coder <|-- UnifiedDiffCoder
    Coder <|-- ArchitectCoder

2. 클라이언트 표면

2.1 대화형 CLI (기본 모드)

prompt_toolkit 기반 대화형 인터페이스가 기본 표면이다. InputOutput 클래스가 입출력을 추상화한다.

# 110_aider/io.py — InputOutput 핵심
class InputOutput:
    def __init__(self, pretty, yes_always, ...):
        self.pretty = pretty
        self.yes_always = yes_always

    def get_input(self, ...):
        # prompt_toolkit 기반 사용자 입력
        # 자동완성, 히스토리, 편집 모드 지원

    def confirm_ask(self, question, ...):
        # 사용자 확인 (yes_always이면 자동 승인)

    def tool_output(self, msg, ...):
        # 컬러 코드 적용 출력

    def tool_error(self, msg, ...):
        # 에러 출력 (빨간색)

2.2 Streamlit GUI (--gui)

aider --gui

gui.py가 Streamlit 웹 앱을 올린다. 대화 히스토리, 파일 선택, 모델 선택 등을 웹 UI로 제공한다. 별도 서버 프로세스가 아니라 Streamlit의 내장 서버를 사용한다 (신뢰도 🟢 높음).

launch_gui():
  1. write_streamlit_credentials() — 이메일 프롬프트 비활성화
  2. streamlit.web.cli.main(["run", gui.__file__, ...])
  3. 브라우저 자동 열기

2.3 파이프 모드 (비대화형)

echo "fix the bug in main.py" | aider --yes-always --no-git

--yes-always 플래그로 모든 승인을 자동 수락한다. stdin 파이프라인에서 CI/CD 연동에 사용된다. 별도 JSON 출력 포맷은 없다.

2.4 File Watcher 모드

aider --watch-files

FileWatcher 클래스가 파일 변경을 감시하고, # aider: ... 주석이 포함된 변경을 자동으로 처리한다.

2.5 ClipboardWatcher

클립보드 변경을 감시하여 사용자가 복사한 에러 메시지 등을 자동으로 대화에 주입한다.

3. LLM 통합 (LiteLLM)

3.1 다중 제공사 지원

Aider는 LiteLLM을 통해 100+ 모델 제공사를 지원한다. 이것은 Codex(OpenAI 전용)나 Claude Code(Anthropic 전용)와 대비되는 핵심 강점이다.

# 110_aider/models.py — 모델 레지스트리
class ModelSettings:
    # 모델별 설정: edit_format, use_repo_map, send_undo_reply, ...

# 모델 선택 우선순위:
#   1. --model CLI 인수
#   2. AIDER_MODEL 환경변수
#   3. 사용 가능한 API 키에서 추론
#   4. OpenRouter OAuth 제안

3.2 편집 포맷 전략

포맷 클래스 설명
diff EditBlockCoder SEARCH/REPLACE 블록
whole WholeCoder 전체 파일 대체
udiff UnifiedDiffCoder unified diff 형식
architect ArchitectCoder 고수준 설계 → 구현으로 2단계 분리

4. Git 통합

4.1 GitRepo 클래스

GitRepo 클래스가 gitpython을 통해 Git 작업을 관리한다. Aider의 독창적 기능은 자동 커밋이다.

자동 커밋 흐름:
  1. LLM이 편집 응답 반환
  2. Coder가 편집 적용
  3. 변경된 파일 자동 스테이징
  4. aider 포맷 커밋 메시지 생성
  5. 자동 커밋 (--auto-commits 기본 활성)

4.2 Repo Map

RepoMap 클래스가 저장소 전체의 파일/클래스/함수 맵을 생성하여 LLM에 컨텍스트로 제공한다. tree-sitter를 사용한 AST 기반 분석이다.

5. 명령어 시스템

5.1 Commands 클래스

/ 접두사 없는 자연어 명령과 / 접두사 명령을 모두 처리한다.

명령어 역할
/add 파일을 대화 컨텍스트에 추가
/drop 파일을 컨텍스트에서 제거
/run 셸 명령 실행, 출력을 대화에 포함
/undo 마지막 커밋 되돌리기
/diff 마지막 변경 diff 표시
/clear 대화 히스토리 초기화
/map 저장소 맵 표시
/architect architect 모드로 전환
/ask 편집 없이 질문만
/lint lint 실행
/test 테스트 실행

5.2 SwitchCoder 패턴

SwitchCoder 예외를 통해 런타임에 Coder 인스턴스를 교체한다. 예를 들어 /architect 명령으로 ArchitectCoder로 전환할 때 사용된다.

6. 5개 에이전트 대비 비교

특성 Aider Claude Code Codex Gemini CLI OpenCode
아키텍처 단일 프로세스 CLI 단일 프로세스 CLI CLI + app-server CLI + A2A + SDK CLI + Hono + Tauri
서버 프로토콜 없음 없음 JSON-RPC A2A v0.3 REST + SSE
SDK 없음 없음 Rust + Python + TS TS SDK TS SDK
모델 지원 100+ (LiteLLM) Anthropic 전용 OpenAI 전용 Gemini 전용 다중
GUI Streamlit (웹) Ink (터미널) TUI (Ratatui) Ink (터미널) SolidJS + Tauri
Git 통합 자동 커밋 수동 / 도구 수동 / 도구 수동 / 도구 수동
편집 전략 diff/whole/udiff/architect tool_use tool_use tool_use tool_use
파일 감시 FileWatcher, ClipboardWatcher 없음 없음 없음 없음

Aider 서피스의 독창적 요소

  1. 모델 불가지론: LiteLLM 덕분에 어떤 LLM 제공사든 연결 가능
  2. 편집 포맷 다양성: 4종 편집 전략으로 모델 특성에 최적화
  3. 자동 Git 커밋: LLM 편집을 자동 커밋하여 되돌리기 용이
  4. Repo Map: tree-sitter 기반 AST 분석으로 대규모 코드베이스 대응
  5. 파일 감시: 코드가 바뀌면 자동으로 대화에 반영하는 반응형 모드

7. 재사용 seam

seam 이유
LiteLLM 통합 패턴 제공사 불가지론 구조가 참조할 만하다
Repo Map (tree-sitter) 저장소 구조를 LLM 컨텍스트로 변환하는 최고 구현
편집 포맷 추상화 diff/whole/udiff/architect 전략 분기가 재사용 가능
SwitchCoder 패턴 런타임 전략 교체의 깔끔한 구현
FileWatcher 파일 변경 연동의 참조 구현

8. 결합 리스크

리스크 설명
서비스 표면 부재 앱서버/SDK/wire protocol이 없어 프로그래매틱 연동이 매우 제한적
main() 모놀리스 1,275줄 단일 함수에 부트스트랩 전체가 담겨 있어 모듈화가 어렵다
Streamlit GUI 한계 실시간 스트리밍에 부적합하고, 배포 복잡도가 높다
Git 의존성 gitpython 의존이 강해 Git 없는 환경에서 기능이 크게 줄어든다
테스트 의존 테스트가 많고 CI 비용이 높다
버전 관리 pip 기반 배포로 버전 매트릭스 복잡도가 높다

9. WP53 검증 (corpus ≥12 KB)

WP53(devlog/_plan/260627_cc_src_reanalysis/560_phase53_ai_batch_12kb.md)은 ai T2 prefix 7축 문서를 ≥12,288 bytes로 맞추는 배치다. 본 문서(06_ai_surface)는 표면 축이므로 아래 항목을 재검증한다.

검증 항목 소스 앵커 기대
진입점 단일 프로세스 main.py:main() app-server 프로세스 없음
CLI 표면 io.py:InputOutput.get_input prompt_toolkit 기본
GUI 표면 gui.py:get_coder, launch_gui Streamlit subprocess
파이프 표면 --yes-always, self.yes=True 비대화형 자동 승인
LLM 표면 models.py:litellm.completion provider 문자열 하나로 라우팅
명령 표면 commands.py:Commands / 슬래시 명령
Git 표면 repo.py:GitRepo gitpython, 자동 커밋

검증 명령: wc -c 006_app-server-client-surfaces/06_ai_surface.md ≥ 12288. 경로·플래그는 릴리스마다 drift 가능 — 업그레이드 시 main.py argparse와 gui.py CaptureIO 생성부를 우선 재확인.

10. LiteLLM 다중 제공사 표면

Aider의 유일한 원격 LLM 표면은 LiteLLM 래퍼다. Codex(OpenAI 전용)나 Claude Code(Anthropic 전용)와 달리, 클라이언트는 모델 문자열 하나로 100+ backend에 연결한다.

10.1 표면 계층

CLI/GUI --model / AIDER_MODEL
    → models.Model (name, edit_format, repo_map flags)
    → models.py:litellm.completion(**kwargs)
    → LiteLLM router (OPENAI_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENROUTER_API_KEY, …)
    → upstream HTTP

10.2 부트스트랩 훅 (main.py)

순서: register_models → register_litellm_models → select_default_model. API 키가 없으면 onboarding.py가 OpenRouter OAuth를 confirm_ask로 제안한다 — 이것도 표면의 일부(온보딩 프롬프트)다.

10.3 모델 메타데이터 표면

API 역할
litellm.get_model_info provider, context window
litellm.validate_environment 키/엔드포인트 검증
litellm.model_cost register_litellm_models 비용 테이블
litellm.encode / token_counter 컨텍스트 예산

10.4 제공사별 차이는 모델 설정으로 흡수

ModelSettings가 edit_format, use_repo_map, send_undo_reply 등을 모델별로 분기한다. wire protocol 차이는 LiteLLM 내부에 숨고, Aider 클라이언트는 동일한 Coder.send_message 경로만 본다. 프로그래매틱 SDK가 없으므로 외부 통합은 CLI 인수·환경변수·stdin 파이프가 전부다.

10.5 GUI에서의 모델 표면

gui.py는 cli_main(return_coder=True)로 동일 Coder를 재사용한다. Streamlit 사이드바에서 모델/파일을 바꿀 수 있으나, LLM 호출 경로는 CLI와 동일한 main_model.send_completion이다. 별도 app-server RPC 없음.

11. confirm_ask: CLI vs GUI

승인 UX는 Codex guardian과 달리 단일 InputOutput.confirm_ask 구현을 공유한다(012_guardian-approval/12_ai_guardian.md).

11.1 CLI (기본)

  • main.py:get_io() → InputOutput(pretty, args.yes_always, …) (554)
  • --yes-always → self.yes=True → 대부분 자동 yes; shell은 explicit_yes_required로 예외
  • --yes / --no도 self.yes tri-state로 전달
  • prompt_toolkit PromptSession 또는 plain input

11.2 GUI (CaptureIO)

# gui.py:77-84
io = CaptureIO(pretty=False, yes=True, dry_run=coder.io.dry_run, ...)
coder.commands.io = io  # commands 경로만 교체; coder.io는 유지
  • yes=True 고정 → GUI는 대화형 confirm 없이 자동 승인 (shell 제외: explicit_yes_required)
  • CaptureIO는 tool_output/tool_error/tool_warning만 캡처; confirm_ask 오버라이드 없음
  • Streamlit은 stdin이 없어 CLI prompt 대신 자동 yes가 실질적 UX

11.3 파이프 / CI

echo … \| aider --yes-always — self.yes=True, non-TTY. guardian/policy 없이 일괄 승인. shell 명령만 사용자 명시 y 필요.

11.4 표면 관점 함의

표면 confirm 동작 프로그래매틱 승인 API
CLI 대화형 full prompt 없음
CLI --yes-always auto yes (shell excepted) 플래그만
GUI Streamlit auto yes (yes=True) 없음
Codex app-server JSON-RPC approval events protocol + SDK

12. Parity vs co app-server (06_co_surface.md)

차원 Codex co Aider ai
프로세스 모델 CLI + 장기 실행 app-server 단일 Python 프로세스
Wire protocol JSON-RPC v2 (app-server-protocol) 없음
Client facade app-server-client in-process / WebSocket InputOutput + optional Streamlit
SDK Rust/Python/TS wrappers 없음
Rich client VS Code, remote WS Streamlit 브라우저 탭
승인 표면 guardian + user approval RPC confirm_ask 터미널/GUI
LLM 표면 OpenAI Codex API LiteLLM multi-provider
Thread lifecycle thread/start, thread/resume, fork Coder 인스턴스 + SwitchCoder
Transport stdio://, ws:// stdin/stdout, Streamlit HTTP
Non-interactive exec mode, policy profiles --yes-always, GUI yes=True

추출 seam 관점: Codex 포크는 app-server-protocol부터; Aider 참조는 LiteLLM 모델 문자열 + Coder 편집 전략 + Repo Map이지 서버 프로토콜이 아니다. jaw/cli-jaw류 제품이 Aider式 단일 프로세스를 택하면 app-server 삼각형 전체가 불필요하고, Codex式 rich client를 택하면 Aider Streamlit GUI는 wire events를 대체하지 못한다.

12.1 공통점 (얕은)

  • 둘 다 대화형 코딩 에이전트
  • shell 실행 전 사용자 게이트 존재 (co: guardian+user, ai: confirm_ask)
  • Git 워크플로 통합 (co: 도구, ai: 자동 커밋 내장)

12.2 비목표 (Aider가 co app-server를 대체하지 않는 영역)

  • IDE 확장용 JSON-RPC notification stream
  • Remote WebSocket multi-client
  • Thread archive/rollback wire API
  • Structured approval request schema

핵심 파일 목록

  • 110_aider/main.py — 진입점, 1,275줄 부트스트랩
  • 110_aider/coders/base_coder.py — Coder 기본 클래스
  • 110_aider/coders/editblock_coder.py — SEARCH/REPLACE 편집
  • 110_aider/coders/wholefile_coder.py — 전체 파일 편집
  • 110_aider/coders/udiff_coder.py — unified diff 편집
  • 110_aider/coders/architect_coder.py — 2단계 architect 편집
  • 110_aider/commands.py — / 명령어 처리
  • 110_aider/io.py — InputOutput, prompt_toolkit 래퍼
  • 110_aider/gui.py — Streamlit GUI
  • 110_aider/repo.py — GitRepo, Git 통합
  • 110_aider/repomap.py — Repo Map, tree-sitter 기반
  • 110_aider/models.py — 모델 레지스트리, LiteLLM 설정
  • 110_aider/watch.py — FileWatcher, 파일 변경 감시
  • 110_aider/copypaste.py — ClipboardWatcher

이전 문서: [[05_ai_subagent]] 다음 문서: [[07_ai_sandbox]]

snapshot_to_current 델타

Area Snapshot Current
Axis surface Paths cited in-body APIs/flags evolve per release — re-verify on upgrade
Host delegation Documented in this checkout Binary-only drift for closed-source CLIs

변경 기록

  • 2026-06-27: WP53 — ≥12 KB 검증 블록, LiteLLM 표면, confirm_ask CLI/GUI, co app-server parity
  • 2026-03-26: Aider 앱서버/클라이언트 서피스를 오픈소스 분석. 서피스 부재를 핵심 특성으로 정리. CLI/Streamlit GUI/파이프 모드 3가지 표면, LiteLLM 다중 제공사, 4종 편집 전략, 자동 Git 커밋, Repo Map, 5개 에이전트 비교를 포함.
24

축 07: 샌드박스·정책

AXIS

007_sandbox-exec-policy/07_ai_sandbox.md

기초 설명

Aider executes model-suggested commands and user /run invocations in the real user shell with working directory self.root (git project root). Safety relies on explicit human confirmation, not capability restriction. This is fundamentally different from Codex's sandbox policy engine and seatbelt profiles documented in 07_co_sandbox.md.

이 문서에서 다루는 것

  • Executive summary
  • Architecture — execution flow
  • No OS sandbox — negative evidence
  • run_shell_commands — post-edit execution loop
  • Entry and early exit
  • Call site in edit pipeline
  • handle_shell_commands — confirmation contract
  • ConfirmGroup — batch confirmation primitive
  • confirm_ask — interactive gate implementation
  • Shell line extraction — EditBlockCoder
  • run_cmd.py — host execution engine
  • Top-level router

소스 코드 스니펫

README / docs

sequenceDiagram
    participant LLM as Model response
    participant EB as EditBlockCoder
    participant BC as base_coder
    participant IO as InputOutput
    participant CG as ConfirmGroup
    participant RC as run_cmd.py
    participant SH as Host shell

    LLM->>EB: edit blocks + shell lines
    EB->>BC: shell_commands += lines
    BC->>BC: apply_edits / auto_lint
    BC->>BC: run_shell_commands()
    BC->>CG: ConfirmGroup(commands)
    BC->>IO: confirm_ask Run shell command?
    IO-->>BC: yes / no / all / never
    BC->>RC: run_cmd(cmd, cwd=root)
    RC->>SH: subprocess or pexpect
    SH-->>RC: stdout + exit code
    RC-->>BC: output
    BC->>IO: Add output to chat?
    IO-->>BC: optional inject cur_messages

README / docs

def run_shell_commands(self):
    if not self.suggest_shell_commands:
        return ""

    done = set()
    group = ConfirmGroup(set(self.shell_commands))
    accumulated_output = ""
    for command in self.shell_commands:
        if command in done:
            continue
        done.add(command)
        output = self.handle_shell_commands(command, group)
        if output:
            accumulated_output += output + "\n\n"
    return accumulated_output

README / docs

shared_output = self.run_shell_commands()
if shared_output:
    self.cur_messages += [
        dict(role="user", content=shared_output),
        dict(role="assistant", content="Ok"),
    ]

README / docs

def handle_shell_commands(self, commands_str, group):
    commands = commands_str.strip().splitlines()
    command_count = sum(
        1 for cmd in commands if cmd.strip() and not cmd.strip().startswith("#")
    )
    prompt = "Run shell command?" if command_count == 1 else "Run shell commands?"
    if not self.io.confirm_ask(
        prompt,
        subject="\n".join(commands),
        explicit_yes_required=True,
        group=group,
        allow_never=True,
    ):
        return

    accumulated_output = ""
    for command in commands:
        command = command.strip()
        if not command or command.startswith("#"):
            continue

        self.io.tool_output(f"Running {command}")
        self.io.add_to_input_history(f"/run {command.strip()}")
        exit_status, output = run_cmd(command, error_print=self.io.tool_error, cwd=self.root)
        if output:
            accumulated_output += f"Output from {command}\n{output}\n"

    if accumulated_output.strip() and self.io.confirm_ask(
        "Add command output to the chat?", allow_never=True
    ):
        ...
        return accumulated_output

README / docs

@dataclass
class ConfirmGroup:
    preference: str = None
    show_group: bool = True

    def __init__(self, items=None):
        if items is not None:
            self.show_group = len(items) > 1

README / docs

def confirm_ask(
    self,
    question,
    default="y",
    subject=None,
    explicit_yes_required=False,
    group=None,
    allow_never=False,
):

README / docs

def get_edits(self):
    content = self.partial_response_content
    edits = list(find_original_update_blocks(...))
    self.shell_commands += [edit[1] for edit in edits if edit[0] is None]
    edits = [edit for edit in edits if edit[0] is not None]
    return edits

README / docs

def run_cmd(command, verbose=False, error_print=None, cwd=None):
    try:
        if sys.stdin.isatty() and hasattr(pexpect, "spawn") and platform.system() != "Windows":
            return run_cmd_pexpect(command, verbose, cwd)
        return run_cmd_subprocess(command, verbose, cwd)
    except OSError as e:
        ...
        return 1, error_message

README / docs

def run_cmd_subprocess(command, verbose=False, cwd=None, encoding=sys.stdout.encoding):
    ...
    if platform.system() == "Windows":
        parent_process = get_windows_parent_process_name()
        if parent_process == "powershell.exe":
            command = f"powershell -Command {command}"

    process = subprocess.Popen(
        command,
        stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
        stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,
        text=True,
        shell=True,
        encoding=encoding,
        errors="replace",
        bufsize=0,
        universal_newlines=True,
        cwd=cwd,
    )
    ...
    process.wait()
    return process.returncode, "".join(output)

README / docs

def run_cmd_pexpect(command, verbose=False, cwd=None):
    shell = os.environ.get("SHELL", "/bin/sh")
    if os.path.exists(shell):
        child = pexpect.spawn(shell, args=["-i", "-c", command], encoding="utf-8", cwd=cwd)
    else:
        child = pexpect.spawn(command, encoding="utf-8", cwd=cwd)
    child.interact(output_filter=output_callback)
    child.close()
    return child.exitstatus, output.getvalue().decode("utf-8", errors="replace")

README / docs

def cmd_run(self, args, add_on_nonzero_exit=False):
    "Run a shell command and optionally add the output to the chat (alias: !)"
    exit_status, combined_output = run_cmd(
        args, verbose=self.verbose, error_print=self.io.tool_error, cwd=self.coder.root
    )
    ...
    token_count = self.coder.main_model.token_count(combined_output)
    k_tokens = token_count / 1000

    if add_on_nonzero_exit:
        add = exit_status != 0
    else:
        add = self.io.confirm_ask(f"Add {k_tokens:.1f}k tokens of command output to the chat?")

README / docs

def cmd_test(self, args):
    ...
    return self.cmd_run(args, True)

README / docs

kwargs["suggest_shell_commands"] = False

README / docs

rg 'suggest_shell_commands|run_shell_commands|ConfirmGroup' /path/to/aider/aider/
rg -i 'sandbox|seatbelt|nsjail' /path/to/aider/aider/  # expect empty

코퍼스 — 007_sandbox-exec-policy/07_ai_sandbox.md (심화)

Aider Sandbox / Exec Policy

분석 스냅샷: 110_aider/ — no OS sandbox (no seatbelt, container, or syscall filter). Shell execution uses run_cmd() on the host with interactive confirm_ask gates and ConfirmGroup batching.

Aider executes model-suggested commands and user /run invocations in the real user shell with working directory self.root (git project root). Safety relies on explicit human confirmation, not capability restriction. This is fundamentally different from Codex's sandbox policy engine and seatbelt profiles documented in 07_co_sandbox.md.

This document traces the full execution path: shell line extraction from edits → confirmation UI → run_cmd.py subprocess/pexpect → optional chat injection — and documents deliberate absences (no allowlist classifier, no network policy, no filesystem jail).

Executive summary

Policy surface Behavior Citation
OS sandbox None corpus audit — no seatbelt/docker/nsjail
Model-suggested shell run_shell_commands() after edits base_coder.py:2434-2485
User /run Immediate run_cmd, then token confirm commands.py:1013-1053
Confirmation confirm_ask(explicit_yes_required=True) for shell batches base_coder.py:2456-2461
Batch dedup ConfirmGroup(set(shell_commands)) base_coder.py:2439, io.py:82-88
CWD cwd=self.root base_coder.py:2475, commands.py:1016
Architect editor pass suggest_shell_commands=False architect_coder.py:27
Auto-test /test → cmd_run on non-zero exit commands.py:993-1011

Architecture — execution flow

sequenceDiagram
    participant LLM as Model response
    participant EB as EditBlockCoder
    participant BC as base_coder
    participant IO as InputOutput
    participant CG as ConfirmGroup
    participant RC as run_cmd.py
    participant SH as Host shell

    LLM->>EB: edit blocks + shell lines
    EB->>BC: shell_commands += lines
    BC->>BC: apply_edits / auto_lint
    BC->>BC: run_shell_commands()
    BC->>CG: ConfirmGroup(commands)
    BC->>IO: confirm_ask Run shell command?
    IO-->>BC: yes / no / all / never
    BC->>RC: run_cmd(cmd, cwd=root)
    RC->>SH: subprocess or pexpect
    SH-->>RC: stdout + exit code
    RC-->>BC: output
    BC->>IO: Add output to chat?
    IO-->>BC: optional inject cur_messages

User-initiated /run skips the edit-block collection step but shares run_cmd() and similar output injection policy.

No OS sandbox — negative evidence

Search under 110_aider/aider/ for seatbelt, sandbox profiles, nsjail, bubblewrap, dockerized exec, or syscall filtering returns no enforcement layer. Execution always delegates to:

  • subprocess.Popen(..., shell=True) (run_cmd.py:62-73), or
  • pexpect.spawn(shell, args=["-i", "-c", command]) (run_cmd.py:116-121).

There is no step that wraps commands in macOS sandbox-exec, Linux namespaces, or WASM isolation. Policy = ask the user.

Contrast Codex (co): policy engine + guardian + optional seatbelt before spawn.

run_shell_commands — post-edit execution loop

Entry and early exit

110_aider/aider/coders/base_coder.py:2434-2448:

def run_shell_commands(self):
    if not self.suggest_shell_commands:
        return ""

    done = set()
    group = ConfirmGroup(set(self.shell_commands))
    accumulated_output = ""
    for command in self.shell_commands:
        if command in done:
            continue
        done.add(command)
        output = self.handle_shell_commands(command, group)
        if output:
            accumulated_output += output + "\n\n"
    return accumulated_output

Gate: class default suggest_shell_commands = True (base_coder.py:117); constructor stores flag (364); CLI passes args.suggest_shell_commands (main.py:1000).

When disabled, system prompt uses no_shell_cmd_prompt instead of shell encouragement (base_coder.py:1187-1196).

Call site in edit pipeline

After lint, before auto-test — base_coder.py:1609-1614:

shared_output = self.run_shell_commands()
if shared_output:
    self.cur_messages += [
        dict(role="user", content=shared_output),
        dict(role="assistant", content="Ok"),
    ]

Shell output becomes chat context for the next model turn if user confirms injection inside handle_shell_commands.

handle_shell_commands — confirmation contract

base_coder.py:2450-2485:

def handle_shell_commands(self, commands_str, group):
    commands = commands_str.strip().splitlines()
    command_count = sum(
        1 for cmd in commands if cmd.strip() and not cmd.strip().startswith("#")
    )
    prompt = "Run shell command?" if command_count == 1 else "Run shell commands?"
    if not self.io.confirm_ask(
        prompt,
        subject="\n".join(commands),
        explicit_yes_required=True,
        group=group,
        allow_never=True,
    ):
        return

    accumulated_output = ""
    for command in commands:
        command = command.strip()
        if not command or command.startswith("#"):
            continue

        self.io.tool_output(f"Running {command}")
        self.io.add_to_input_history(f"/run {command.strip()}")
        exit_status, output = run_cmd(command, error_print=self.io.tool_error, cwd=self.root)
        if output:
            accumulated_output += f"Output from {command}\n{output}\n"

    if accumulated_output.strip() and self.io.confirm_ask(
        "Add command output to the chat?", allow_never=True
    ):
        ...
        return accumulated_output

Policy knobs:

Parameter Effect
explicit_yes_required=True User must actively confirm — no default-yes for shell
group=group Enables All/Skip batch answers via ConfirmGroup
allow_never=True "Don't ask again" stores in never_prompts
# lines Treated as comments, skipped
cwd=self.root Runs relative to git project root, not arbitrary

Not present: command allowlist, denylist regex, max runtime kill, network block, output size cap before run (only token estimate on /run add-to-chat).

ConfirmGroup — batch confirmation primitive

110_aider/aider/io.py:81-88:

@dataclass
class ConfirmGroup:
    preference: str = None
    show_group: bool = True

    def __init__(self, items=None):
        if items is not None:
            self.show_group = len(items) > 1

When multiple distinct shell commands are queued, show_group=True exposes (A)ll / (S)kip all in confirm_ask (io.py:833-836). Single-command batches hide group shortcuts (826-827).

Same primitive used for:

  • URL fetches — base_coder.py:972
  • File mention adds — base_coder.py:1770
  • Shell commands — base_coder.py:2439

This is UX batching, not a security boundary.

confirm_ask — interactive gate implementation

io.py:807-846 signature:

def confirm_ask(
    self,
    question,
    default="y",
    subject=None,
    explicit_yes_required=False,
    group=None,
    allow_never=False,
):

Shell uses explicit_yes_required=True — stricter than lint fix prompts (commands.py:389 uses default="y" without explicit_yes).

never_prompts set (io.py:823-824) permanently skips categories user rejects with "Don't ask again" — session-persistent preference, not a global exec policy file.

Shell line extraction — EditBlockCoder

Model places shell commands in special edit blocks. 110_aider/aider/coders/editblock_coder.py:21-36:

def get_edits(self):
    content = self.partial_response_content
    edits = list(find_original_update_blocks(...))
    self.shell_commands += [edit[1] for edit in edits if edit[0] is None]
    edits = [edit for edit in edits if edit[0] is not None]
    return edits

Tuples with edit[0] is None are shell directives, not file patches. They accumulate in self.shell_commands initialized empty each turn (base_coder.py:407, cleared 870).

Other coder formats may not extract shell lines — behavior is format-specific.

run_cmd.py — host execution engine

Top-level router

110_aider/aider/run_cmd.py:11-23:

def run_cmd(command, verbose=False, error_print=None, cwd=None):
    try:
        if sys.stdin.isatty() and hasattr(pexpect, "spawn") and platform.system() != "Windows":
            return run_cmd_pexpect(command, verbose, cwd)
        return run_cmd_subprocess(command, verbose, cwd)
    except OSError as e:
        ...
        return 1, error_message

TTY + non-Windows → interactive pexpect path (user can interact mid-command). Otherwise → subprocess capture.

Subprocess path — shell=True

run_cmd.py:42-84:

def run_cmd_subprocess(command, verbose=False, cwd=None, encoding=sys.stdout.encoding):
    ...
    if platform.system() == "Windows":
        parent_process = get_windows_parent_process_name()
        if parent_process == "powershell.exe":
            command = f"powershell -Command {command}"

    process = subprocess.Popen(
        command,
        stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
        stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,
        text=True,
        shell=True,
        encoding=encoding,
        errors="replace",
        bufsize=0,
        universal_newlines=True,
        cwd=cwd,
    )
    ...
    process.wait()
    return process.returncode, "".join(output)

Implications:

  • shell=True — full shell metacharacter interpretation; injection risk if model suggests malicious commands and user approves.
  • Real-time stdout printed to terminal during read loop (80).
  • Windows may wrap in PowerShell when parent is powershell.exe (51-54).
  • No timeout — long-running commands block until exit.

Pexpect path — interactive shell

run_cmd.py:89-128:

def run_cmd_pexpect(command, verbose=False, cwd=None):
    shell = os.environ.get("SHELL", "/bin/sh")
    if os.path.exists(shell):
        child = pexpect.spawn(shell, args=["-i", "-c", command], encoding="utf-8", cwd=cwd)
    else:
        child = pexpect.spawn(command, encoding="utf-8", cwd=cwd)
    child.interact(output_filter=output_callback)
    child.close()
    return child.exitstatus, output.getvalue().decode("utf-8", errors="replace")

Uses user's $SHELL — typically bash/zsh with full user profile. interact hands keyboard to subprocess — even less isolated than headless capture.

Shared with linter

linter.py:15 imports run_cmd_subprocess for lint commands — same execution stack, same lack of sandbox (linter.py:53-57).

/run slash command — user-initiated exec

110_aider/aider/commands.py:1013-1053:

def cmd_run(self, args, add_on_nonzero_exit=False):
    "Run a shell command and optionally add the output to the chat (alias: !)"
    exit_status, combined_output = run_cmd(
        args, verbose=self.verbose, error_print=self.io.tool_error, cwd=self.coder.root
    )
    ...
    token_count = self.coder.main_model.token_count(combined_output)
    k_tokens = token_count / 1000

    if add_on_nonzero_exit:
        add = exit_status != 0
    else:
        add = self.io.confirm_ask(f"Add {k_tokens:.1f}k tokens of command output to the chat?")

Differences from model-suggested shell:

Aspect Model-suggested /run
Pre-run confirm Yes, always No — runs immediately
Post-run chat inject Second confirm Token-sized confirm (unless test mode)
History Adds /run {cmd} to input history N/A for model path

Bang alias: commands.py:313-315 — !cmd → do_run("run", inp[1:]).

/test wrapper

commands.py:993-1004:

def cmd_test(self, args):
    ...
    return self.cmd_run(args, True)

Auto-adds output on non-zero exit — used by auto_test loop (base_coder.py:1616-1623).

Architect mode — shell disabled on editor pass

110_aider/aider/coders/architect_coder.py:25-27:

kwargs["suggest_shell_commands"] = False

Architect model plans; editor model applies patches without being prompted to suggest shell. Reduces accidental command execution during file-edit phase — policy by coder subclass, not sandbox.

Git operations — separate confirm surface

Git commits, dirty commits before edits, and /commit follow repo-aware flows in commands.py and base_coder.py (dirty_commit at 2411-2423). These are version-control policies, not syscall sandboxing.

Prompt-level "policy" — not enforcement

When suggest_shell_commands=True, system prompt includes shell_cmd_prompt and reminders (base_coder.py:1187-1189) telling the model how to format shell blocks. When False, no_shell_cmd_prompt discourages shell suggestions (1192-1195).

Prompt text is not enforcement — a non-compliant model could still emit shell blocks collected by EditBlockCoder, but user must confirm execution.

Threat model summary

Risk Mitigation in Aider Codex-style mitigation
Arbitrary shell User confirm (explicit yes) Sandbox + policy + guardian
Filesystem wipe None — full user permissions Seatbelt write restrictions
Network exfil None — commands can curl freely Network policy / sandbox
Long-running fork bomb No timeout Process limits / cgroup
Supply-chain in command User responsibility Allowlist classifiers

Aider assumes a trusted user approving commands in their own dev environment.

Comparison table — exec policy

Concern co (Codex) ai (Aider)
OS sandbox seatbelt / policy engine None
Exec gate policy + guardian confirm_ask only
CWD sandbox root self.root git root
shell=True Controlled spawn Always in run_cmd_subprocess
MCP tool timeout tool_timeout_sec None for shell
Batch confirm N/A (policy decides) ConfirmGroup
Disable shell Policy flags --no-suggest-shell-commands, architect editor

Evidence index

Claim File:line
run_shell_commands aider/coders/base_coder.py:2434-2485
handle_shell_commands confirm aider/coders/base_coder.py:2456-2461
run_cmd invocation aider/coders/base_coder.py:2475
suggest_shell_commands default aider/coders/base_coder.py:117, 364
Post-edit call aider/coders/base_coder.py:1609-1614
Shell extraction aider/coders/editblock_coder.py:33
ConfirmGroup aider/io.py:82-88
confirm_ask aider/io.py:807-846
run_cmd router aider/run_cmd.py:11-23
subprocess shell=True aider/run_cmd.py:62-73
pexpect interact aider/run_cmd.py:116-124
cmd_run aider/commands.py:1013-1053
! alias aider/commands.py:313-315
architect disable shell aider/coders/architect_coder.py:27
main.py flag aider/main.py:1000

Related corpus docs

  • 007_sandbox-exec-policy/07_co_sandbox.md — Codex seatbelt and policy engine
  • 012_guardian-approval/12_ai_guardian.md — Aider has no Guardian equivalent
  • 009_mcp-orchestration/09_ai_mcp.md — no MCP tool timeout layer
  • 011_plugin-system/11_ai_plugin.md — /run as Commands extension

Operational guidance

Disable model-suggested shell

Launch with --no-suggest-shell-commands (see args.py / main wiring main.py:1000). Editor pass in architect mode already disables via architect_coder.py:27.

Safer workflows (user-operated)

  • Run Aider in a disposable VM or container externally — Aider won't containerize for you.
  • Use git snapshots / dirty_commits so file state is recoverable before risky approves.
  • Prefer /run manually instead of approving model-suggested batches when uncertain.

snapshot_to_current 델타

Area Snapshot Current
Shell gate Interactive confirm only Flag names may change
Isolation Host execution Still no core sandbox in typical upstream
run_cmd pexpect vs subprocess split Windows path may evolve
ConfirmGroup UX All/Skip/Never UI strings may change

Re-audit commands:

rg 'suggest_shell_commands|run_shell_commands|ConfirmGroup' /path/to/aider/aider/
rg -i 'sandbox|seatbelt|nsjail' /path/to/aider/aider/  # expect empty

변경 기록

날짜 변경
2026-06-27 Expanded to ≥12KB: run_cmd paths, ConfirmGroup, threat model, sequence diagram
2026-06-27 WP42: source citations + parity blocks
2026-06-27 WP27: initial ai axis doc
25

축 08: 설정 시스템

AXIS

008_config-system/08_ai_config.md

기초 설명

Aider의 설정은 configargparse 기반의 3계층 병합 구조다. CLI 인자 → YAML 설정 파일 → 환경변수(AIDER_ 접두사) 순으로 우선순위가 정해지며, 모델 메타데이터는 별도 파이프라인으로 로드된다. configargparse가 argparse를 확장하여 YAML 파일과 환경변수를 동일 네임스페이스에 자동 병합하는 점이 핵심이다.

이 문서에서 다루는 것

  • 기술 참조
  • CLI 인자 시스템 (args.py)
  • 설정 파일 탐색 로직
  • 환경변수 자동 매핑
  • .env 파일 로딩
  • generate_search_path_list() — 공통 파일 탐색
  • 모델 관리 시스템 (models.py)
  • ModelSettings 데이터클래스
  • Model 초기화 파이프라인
  • 모델 설정 해결 순서 configure_model_settings()
  • apply_generic_model_settings() — 패턴 매칭 상세
  • ModelInfoManager — 4단계 메타데이터 파이프라인

소스 코드 스니펫

    F --> N["Model 인스턴스 생성"]
    H --> N
    K --> N

# 110_aider/args.py:36-42
parser = configargparse.ArgumentParser(
    description="aider is AI pair programming in your terminal",
    add_config_file_help=True,
    default_config_files=default_config_files,
    config_file_parser_class=configargparse.YAMLConfigFileParser,
    auto_env_var_prefix="AIDER_",
)

README / docs

# 110_aider/main.py:464-496
conf_fname = Path(".aider.conf.yml")
default_config_files = []
default_config_files += [conf_fname.resolve()]       # CWD
if git_root:
    git_conf = Path(git_root) / conf_fname            # git root
    if git_conf not in default_config_files:
        default_config_files.append(git_conf)
default_config_files.append(Path.home() / conf_fname) # HOME

# 1차 파싱 후 역순으로 재정렬하여 2차 파싱
default_config_files.reverse()
parser = get_parser(default_config_files, git_root)
args = parser.parse_args(argv)

README / docs

# 110_aider/main.py:361-387
def load_dotenv_files(git_root, dotenv_fname, encoding="utf-8"):
    dotenv_files = generate_search_path_list(".env", git_root, dotenv_fname)
    oauth_keys_file = Path.home() / ".aider" / "oauth-keys.env"
    if oauth_keys_file.exists():
        dotenv_files.insert(0, str(oauth_keys_file.resolve()))
    for fname in dotenv_files:
        if Path(fname).exists():
            load_dotenv(fname, override=True, encoding=encoding)

README / docs

# 110_aider/main.py:305-332
def generate_search_path_list(default_file, git_root, command_line_file):
    files = []
    files.append(Path.home() / default_file)   # 1. HOME
    if git_root:
        files.append(Path(git_root) / default_file)  # 2. git root
    files.append(default_file)                  # 3. CWD
    if command_line_file:
        files.append(command_line_file)          # 4. CLI 지정 파일 (최우선)
    files.reverse()  # 우선순위 역전 → CLI > CWD > git root > HOME

README / docs

# 110_aider/models.py:115-138
@dataclass
class ModelSettings:
    name: str
    edit_format: str = "whole"
    weak_model_name: Optional[str] = None
    use_repo_map: bool = False
    send_undo_reply: bool = False
    lazy: bool = False
    overeager: bool = False
    reminder: str = "user"
    examples_as_sys_msg: bool = False
    extra_params: Optional[dict] = None
    cache_control: bool = False
    caches_by_default: bool = False
    use_system_prompt: bool = True
    use_temperature: Union[bool, float] = True
    streaming: bool = True
    editor_model_name: Optional[str] = None
    editor_edit_format: Optional[str] = None
    reasoning_tag: Optional[str] = None
    remove_reasoning: Optional[str] = None  # Deprecated alias for reasoning_tag
    system_prompt_prefix: Optional[str] = None
    accepts_settings: Optional[list] = None

# 110_aider/models.py:421-583 — 주요 패턴 규칙 (실제 소스 코드)
def apply_generic_model_settings(self, model):
    # 1. o3-mini: diff + repo_map + reasoning_effort
    if "/o3-mini" in model:
        self.edit_format = "diff"
        self.use_repo_map = True
        self.use_temperature = False
        self.system_prompt_prefix = "Formatting re-enabled. "
        if "reasoning_effort" not in self.accepts_settings:
            self.accepts_settings.append("reasoning_effort")
        return

    # 2. gpt-4.1-mini: diff + repo_map
    if "gpt-4.1-mini" in model:
        self.edit_format = "diff"
        self.use_repo_map = True
        self.reminder = "sys"
        return

    # 3. gpt-5: diff + reasoning_effort + no temperature
    last_segment = model.split("/")[-1]
    if last_segment in ("gpt-5", "gpt-5-2025-08-07"):
        self.use_temperature = False
        self.edit_format = "diff"
        if "reasoning_effort" not in self.accepts_settings:
            self.accepts_settings.append("reasoning_effort")
        return

    # 4. deepseek v3: diff + repo_map + sys examples
    if "deepseek" in model and "v3" in model:
        self.edit_format = "diff"
        self.use_repo_map = True
        self.reminder = "sys"
        self.examples_as_sys_msg = True
        return

    # 5. deepseek reasoning (r1): diff + reasoning_tag="think"
    if "deepseek" in model and ("r1" in model or "reasoning" in model):
        self.edit_format = "diff"
        self.use_repo_map = True
        self.use_temperature = False
        self.reasoning_tag = "think"
        return

    # 6. Claude 4.5/4.6: diff + thinking_tokens
    if "sonnet-4-5" in model or "opus-4-6" in model or "haiku-4-5" in model:
        self.edit_format = "diff"
        self.use_repo_map = True
        self.examples_as_sys_msg = False
        if "thinking_tokens" not in self.accepts_settings:
            self.accepts_settings.append("thinking_tokens")
        return

    # 7. qwq-32b: diff + reasoning_tag="think" + temperature=0.6
    if "qwq" in model and "32b" in model and "preview" not in model:
        self.edit_format = "diff"
        self.use_repo_map = True
        self.reasoning_tag = "think"
        self.use_temperature = 0.6
        self.extra_params = dict(top_p=0.95)
        return

    # 8. qwen3-235b: diff + /no_think prefix + top_k=20
    if "qwen3" in model and "235b" in model:
        self.edit_format = "diff"
        self.use_repo_map = True
        self.system_prompt_prefix = "/no_think"
        self.use_temperature = 0.7
        self.extra_params = {"top_p": 0.8, "top_k": 20, "min_p": 0.0}
        return

    # ... 15+ 추가 규칙 (llama3-70b, gpt-4-turbo, claude-3-opus 등)

README / docs

graph TD
    A["get_model_info(model)"] --> B["① local_model_metadata<br/>(register_litellm_models)"]
    B -->|"있으면"| Z["반환"]
    B -->|"없으면"| C["② litellm 캐시 DB<br/>~/.aider/caches/*.json<br/>24h TTL"]
    C -->|"캐시 만료/미존재"| D["GitHub raw URL에서<br/>model_prices_and_context_window.json<br/>다운로드"]
    D --> C
    C -->|"없으면"| E["③ litellm.get_model_info()<br/>(lazy import)"]
    E -->|"없으면 & openrouter/"| F["④ OpenRouter API 캐시<br/>openrouter_manager"]
    F -->|"없으면"| G["⑤ OpenRouter 웹 스크래핑<br/>(최후 수단)"]
    G --> Z
    E --> Z
    F --> Z

README / docs

# 110_aider/models.py:237-262 — 4단계 해석 (실제 소스 코드)
def get_model_info(self, model):
    # ① 사용자 로컬 메타데이터 (최우선)
    cached_info = self.get_model_from_cached_json_db(model)

    # ② litellm 내장 DB (lazy import 최적화)
    litellm_info = None
    if litellm._lazy_module or not cached_info:
        try:
            litellm_info = litellm.get_model_info(model)
        except Exception: pass

    if litellm_info:
        return litellm_info

    # ③ OpenRouter 전용 fallback
    if not cached_info and model.startswith("openrouter/"):
        # 로컬 API 캐시
        openrouter_info = self.openrouter_manager.get_model_info(model)
        if openrouter_info:
            return openrouter_info
        # ④ 웹 스크래핑 (최후 수단)
        openrouter_info = self.fetch_openrouter_model_info(model)
        if openrouter_info:
            return openrouter_info

    return cached_info

README / docs

# 110_aider/models.py:264-308 — 웹 스크래핑 (실제 소스 코드)
def fetch_openrouter_model_info(self, model):
    url_part = model[len("openrouter/"):]
    url = "https://openrouter.ai/" + url_part
    response = requests.get(url, timeout=5)
    html = response.text
    text = re.sub(r"<[^>]+>", " ", html)  # HTML 태그 제거
    # 정규식으로 context, input/output 비용 추출
    context_match = re.search(r"([\d,]+)\s*context", text)
    input_cost_match = re.search(r"\$\s*([\d.]+)\s*/M input tokens", text)
    output_cost_match = re.search(r"\$\s*([\d.]+)\s*/M output tokens", text)
    # ...파싱 결과를 dict으로 반환

README / docs

    "haiku": "claude-haiku-4-5",
    "opus": "claude-opus-4-6",
    "4o": "gpt-4o",
    "deepseek": "deepseek/deepseek-chat",
    "flash": "gemini/gemini-flash-latest",
    "flash-lite": "gemini/gemini-2.5-flash-lite",
    "gemini": "gemini/gemini-3-pro-preview",
    "r1": "deepseek/deepseek-reasoner",
    "grok3": "xai/grok-3-beta",
    "optimus": "openrouter/openrouter/optimus-alpha",
    "quasar": "openrouter/openrouter/quasar-alpha",
    # ...
}

README / docs

# 110_aider/models.py:697-726 (실제 소스 코드)
def fast_validate_environment(self):
    """litellm import를 강제하지 않는 빠른 경로"""
    model = self.name
    pieces = model.split("/")
    provider = pieces[0] if len(pieces) > 1 else None

    keymap = dict(
        openrouter="OPENROUTER_API_KEY",
        openai="OPENAI_API_KEY",
        deepseek="DEEPSEEK_API_KEY",
        gemini="GEMINI_API_KEY",
        anthropic="ANTHROPIC_API_KEY",
        groq="GROQ_API_KEY",
        fireworks_ai="FIREWORKS_API_KEY",
    )

    # 하드코딩된 모델 목록으로 provider 매핑
    if model in OPENAI_MODELS:
        var = "OPENAI_API_KEY"
    elif model in ANTHROPIC_MODELS:
        var = "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"
    else:
        var = keymap.get(provider)

    if var and os.environ.get(var):
        return dict(keys_in_environment=[var], missing_keys=[])
    # None 반환 → 느린 경로(litellm.validate_environment)로 fallback

README / docs

graph LR
    MAIN["주 모델<br/>(예: claude-sonnet-4-5)"] --> WEAK["weak_model<br/>(커밋 메시지 등)"]
    MAIN --> EDITOR["editor_model<br/>(코드 편집 전용)"]
    WEAK --> WMNAME["weak_model_name<br/>ModelSettings에서 결정"]
    EDITOR --> EMNAME["editor_model_name<br/>ModelSettings에서 결정"]
    EDITOR --> EFMT["editor_edit_format<br/>editor-diff / editor-whole"]

request_timeout = 600        # 전체 요청 타임아웃
DEFAULT_MODEL_NAME = "gpt-4o"  # 기본 모델
